samples.gvd:
module fsm
(
    input IN,
    input CLK,
    input RST,
    output reg MATCH
);

reg [2:0] state, next_state;
parameter S0 = 3'b000, S1 = 3'b001, S2 = 3'b010, S3 = 3'b011, S4 = 3'b100, S5 = 3'b101, S6 = 3'b110, S7 = 3'b111;

always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
    if (RST) begin
        state <= S0;
        MATCH <= 1'b0;
    end
    else begin
        state <= next_state;
        case (state)
            S0: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S0;
                end
            end
            S1: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S2;
                end
            end
            S2: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S3;
                end
            end
            S3: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S4;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S0;
                end
            end
            S4: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state <= S1;
                    MATCH <= 1'b1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state <= S0;
                end
            end
            default: begin
                next_state <= S0;
            end
        endcase
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
```xml
<module id="fsm">
    <description>Mealy FSM detection circuit that detects the sequence '10011' in the input IN. Outputs MATCH=1 when the sequence is detected, and MATCH=0 otherwise.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Single-bit input signal.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal to initialize the FSM.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating a match condition.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state name="S0" value="000000">Initial state.</state>
        <state name="S1" value="000001">State after detecting '1'.</state>
        <state name="S2" value="000010">State after detecting '10'.</state>
        <state name="S3" value="000011">State after detecting '100'.</state>
        <state name="S4" value="000100">State after detecting '1001'.</state>
        <state name="S5" value="000101">State after detecting '10011'.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <register id="current_state" width="6" initial_value="S0">Current state of the FSM.</register>
        <wire id="next_state" width="6">Next state of the FSM.</wire>
        <assign next_state = {current_state[5], current_state[4], current_state[3], current_state[2], current_state[1], current_state[0]} + ({current_state[5], current_state[4], current_state[3], current_state[2], current_state[1], current_state[0]} << 1) + ({current_state[5], current_state[4], current_state[3], current_state[2], current_state[1], current_state[0]} << 2) + ({current_state[5], current_state[4], current_state[3], current_state[2], current_state[1], current_state[0]} << 3) + ({current_state[5], current_state[4], current_state[3], current_state[2], current_state[1], current_state[0]} << 4) + ({current_state[5], current_state[4], current_state[3], current_state[2], current_state[1], current_state[0]} << 5);</assign>
        <always @(posedge CLK or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                current_state <= S0;
            end else begin
                current_state <= next_state;
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        Always @(*) begin
            case(current_state)
                S0: begin
                    if (IN == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = S1;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                    end
                end
                S1: begin
                    if (IN == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = S2;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                    end
                end
                S2: begin
                    if (IN == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = S3;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                    end
                end
                S3: begin
                    if (IN == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = S4;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                    end
                end
                S4: begin
                    if (IN == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = S5;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                    end
                end
                S5: begin
                    if (IN == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = S0;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                    end
                end
                default: begin
                    next_state = S0;
                end
            endcase
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
```
